medications.indication_rationale: First-line for tinea corporis; apply to the lesions and ~2cm of surrounding normal skin twice daily for ~2 weeks (continue ~1 week after clearing). Source used terbinafine 1% cream.
medications.dose_route_frequency: apply to lesions + a 2cm margin twice daily for ~2 weeks, continuing ~1 week after clearance (as per source note)

medications.drug_name: oral antifungal (e.g. terbinafine/griseofulvin) for limited skin tinea
medications.indication_rationale: Not needed for limited tinea corporis; reserve for scalp/kerion/extensive/refractory disease.
Steroids cause tinea incognito (worsening, atypical, masked infection) — avoid on tinea.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Keep the skin dry, wear loose cotton clothing, use a dedicated towel for the area and hot-wash it after each use, don't share towels/brushes, and have the kitten checked/treated by a vet.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reduces fungal growth and household/zoonotic transmission.

patient_education_points.point: This is ringworm (a skin fungal infection), likely from the new kitten. An antifungal cream clears it — apply it to the ring AND a margin of normal skin, and keep going for a week after it looks clear so it doesn't come back.

Don't use steroid creams on it (they make fungal infections worse and harder to recognise). Keep the skin dry, use a separate towel and hot-wash it, don't share towels, and get the kitten checked by a vet.
See a doctor if it involves your scalp, forms a boggy painful lump, becomes very widespread, isn't improving after a week of the cream, or develops painful blisters, hot painful skin or a fever.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Response; oral antifungal / KOH if extensive/refractory/scalp involvement.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Scalp/kerion/extensive disease or secondary infection — GP.

must_include_items: Recognise tinea corporis; topical antifungal to lesion + margin, continued after clearing
Contagion/zoonosis measures (treat the kitten; towel/fomite hygiene)
acceptable_alternatives: Terbinafine or an azole (e.g. clotrimazole/miconazole) topical
errors_of_commission: Recommending a topical steroid / steroid-antifungal combination
Oral antifungals for limited skin tinea (over-treatment)
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires topical antifungal management, avoiding topical steroids, contagion/zoonosis measures, and an oral-antifungal/scalp safety-net.
